it_director_webmaster.theme: Time, manual labor, technical debt, tool fatigue
pain_points: Manual remediation eating up staff time
Too many tools and not enough integration
New content breaking compliance as fast as they fix it
No bandwidth for PDF backlog
discovery_questions: How much of your team's time is going into accessibility remediation right now?
Are you fixing things manually or do you have something automated?
What's the PDF situation look like — are those being handled too?
How does your current tool integrate with your CMS?
it_director_webmaster.pivot_to: Auto-Remediation and CMS integrations. Emphasize that EqualGround removes the manual burden and plugs directly into their existing stack.

compliance_officer.theme: Audit readiness, tracking progress, fear of non-compliance, documentation
pain_points: Lack of visibility into current compliance status
No audit trail or reporting for leadership
Can't prove compliance to DOJ if investigated
No centralized dashboard across all digital properties
discovery_questions: Do you have visibility right now into where you stand compliance-wise?
If the DOJ came knocking tomorrow, could you show them a compliance report?
Are you tracking remediation progress anywhere, or is it more ad hoc?
Who else is involved in the compliance decision — IT, legal?
compliance_officer.pivot_to: Executive Reporting and Audit-ready dashboards. Emphasize EqualGround generates compliance reports they can hand to leadership or DOJ.

superintendent_city_manager.theme: Budget efficiency, risk mitigation, avoiding DOJ lawsuits, leadership accountability
pain_points: Fear of DOJ lawsuit or investigation
Budget constraints but can't afford non-compliance
Need to show the board/council they're taking action
Don't understand the technical details but know it's a risk
discovery_questions: Is this something that's come up at the board or council level yet?
What's the bigger concern right now — the budget side or the compliance risk side?
Has anyone in your area been hit with a DOJ complaint or lawsuit on this?
Who on your team is closest to the technical side of this?
superintendent_city_manager.pivot_to: Cost of a DOJ lawsuit vs. cost of proactive compliance. Emphasize risk mitigation and that EqualGround is a fraction of the cost of a single lawsuit.

small_county_no_it.theme: Simplicity, automation, affordability, no technical expertise available
pain_points: No dedicated IT staff — one person handles everything
Annual tech budget under $15,000
50+ non-technical department heads managing web content
Using a managed CMS like CivicPlus — cannot modify code
Needs the platform to do the work, not just flag issues
discovery_questions: How many people on your team are handling the web and document side of things?
Are you using a managed platform like CivicPlus where your vendor controls the code?
What does your annual technology budget look like for something like this?
Is the concern more about the document backlog or the ongoing web scanning?
small_county_no_it.pivot_to: Autopilot and simplicity. Do not demo the full platform. Focus on: scan runs automatically, Autopilot fixes what it can, flags the rest with suggested fixes. Non-technical team does not need to touch code. Scope-based pricing means they only pay for their actual footprint — 100 pages and 5,000 docs is a very manageable quote.

commissioner_driven_buyer.theme: Board approval required, needs quote format for non-technical presentation, real deadline driven by meeting schedule
pain_points: Cannot make purchase decisions alone — needs board or commissioners approval
Needs a quote in a specific format to present at an upcoming meeting
Board members are non-technical — needs simple ROI framing not feature lists
Meeting schedule creates a real hard deadline for the quote
discovery_questions: Do you have a commissioners or board meeting coming up where this would need to be presented?
What does the board typically need to see to approve something like this — is it mainly the cost and compliance risk?
Who else besides you would be involved in making this decision?
What is the timeline you are working with to have something ready for them?
commissioner_driven_buyer.pivot_to: Create urgency around the board meeting date. Offer to prepare the quote in a format the rep can present directly to non-technical commissioners — cost, compliance risk, deadline context, and ROI framing. Ask for the meeting date and work backwards. This is a real hard deadline, not a soft one.
